vector_search_best_practices.overview: Best practices for setting up and optimizing vector search systems using Milvus, OpenSearch, and IBM Watsonx embeddings.

principle.name: Choose the Right Vector Database
principle.description: Select vector database based on use case requirements

option.name: opensearch

cons.con: May have higher latency for pure vector search

option.best_for: Systems needing both keyword and semantic search

principle.name: Use IBM Watsonx Embeddings Efficiently
principle.description: Optimize embedding generation for cost and performance

best_practices.practice: Always batch embed documents (100-500 per batch)
Use appropriate model for your use case
Implement retry logic with exponential backoff

principle.name: Embedding Dimension Consistency
principle.description: Ensure dimensions match across all components

validation_checklist.item: Test with sample data before bulk ingestion

principle.name: Optimize HNSW Index Parameters
principle.description: Tune HNSW parameters for your specific workload

parameter.name: M
parameter.description: Number of bi-directional links per node

tuning.guideline: Decrease for lower memory usage (lower recall)

parameter.name: efConstruction
parameter.description: Size of dynamic candidate list during index build

tuning.guideline: Higher = better index quality, slower build

parameter.name: ef
parameter.description: Size of dynamic candidate list during search

tuning.guideline: Start with 100, increase if recall is insufficient

principle.name: Choose Appropriate Distance Metric
principle.description: Select distance metric based on embedding characteristics

metric.name: COSINE
metric.use_case: Most common, works with normalized embeddings

principle.name: Implement Robust Ingestion Pipeline
principle.description: Build reliable data ingestion with error handling

principle.name: Optimize Chunking Strategy
principle.description: Choose appropriate chunking for your documents

principle.name: Implement Hybrid Search
principle.description: Combine vector and keyword search for better results

principle.name: Use Metadata Filtering
principle.description: Narrow search scope with metadata filters
principle.example:
def search_with_filters( query: str, filters: dict, top_k: int = 10 ): """Search with metadata filtering"""
query_vector = embeddings.embed_query(query)
# Build filter expression for Milvus filter_conditions = [] for key, value in filters.items(): if isinstance(value, str): filter_conditions.append(f'{key} == "{value}"') elif isinstance(value, list): values_str = ', '.join([f'"{v}"' for v in value]) filter_conditions.append(f'{key} in [{values_str}]') else: filter_conditions.append(f'{key} == {value}')
filter_expr = " AND ".join(filter_conditions)
results = collection.search( data=[query_vector], anns_field="embedding", param={"metric_type": "COSINE", "params": {"ef": 100}}, limit=top_k, expr=filter_expr, output_fields=["text", "source", "metadata"] )
return results
# Example usage results = search_with_filters( query="machine learning algorithms", filters={ "source": "technical_docs", "language": "en", "year": [2023, 2024] }, top_k=5 )

principle.name: Monitor Vector Search Performance
principle.description: Track key metrics for production systems

pitfall.mistake: Not testing connection before bulk ingestion
pitfall.consequence: Wasted time and resources on failed ingestion
pitfall.solution: Always validate connection and schema before ingestion

pitfall.mistake: Using default HNSW parameters for all workloads
pitfall.solution: Tune parameters based on your recall/latency requirements

pitfall.consequence: Slow ingestion and high API costs
pitfall.solution: Always batch embed and insert operations
Design schema with rich metadata for filtering

principle.name: Secure Credentials Management

best_practices.practice: Use environment variables for all credentials
Use .env files for local development (add to .gitignore)
Use secrets management services in production

principle.example:
# .env.example file MILVUS_HOST=your-milvus-host.com MILVUS_PORT=19530 MILVUS_USER=your-username MILVUS_PASSWORD=your-password WATSONX_URL=https://us-south.ml.cloud.ibm.com WATSONX_API_KEY=your-api-key WATSONX_PROJECT_ID=your-project-id
# Load in code from dotenv import load_dotenv import os
load_dotenv()
# Use environment variables milvus_host = os.getenv("MILVUS_HOST") watsonx_api_key = os.getenv("WATSONX_API_KEY")
